content: Just as the lexical meaning of reflexion encompasses both the physical reflection of light/sound and the cognitive act of deliberate反思 (self-examination), LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to leverage the dual interpretation: 'reflection as representation mirroring' and 'reflection as critical self-examination'. Structured vocabulary acquisition frameworks used to teach the dual sense of reflexion to high school English learners can be repurposed to train LLMs to consistently distinguish between mirroring their existing output and actively critiquing it to improve reasoning.
